title: Dificultatea de a fi Bolojan
summary: Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan faces internal PNL opposition and systematic PSD obstruction in his attempts to reform Romania's administration and reduce government spending. While enjoying public support as an honest reformist, Bolojan risks compromising himself by endlessly accommodating PSD's blocking tactics, and critics argue he lacks political vision beyond accounting efficiency.
talking_points: Bolojan received a near-unanimous confidence vote from PNL's National Political Bureau but failed to secure the two-thirds majority needed to dismiss underperforming county organization leaders, revealing his support is shallow
PSD systematically blocks reform by inventing new legislative packages (spending cuts, economic relaunch, social solidarity) to delay budget adoption and protect local administration spending
The 'corrupted system' including CCR and CAB courts works more efficiently against reform than Bolojan's minority reformist coalition can push for it
Bolojan is described as a capable administrator but not a true political leader — lacking empathy, communication skills, charisma, and a broader national vision beyond fiscal efficiency
Citizens bear the burden of deficit reduction through higher property taxes and income cuts while major tax evaders and political clientele remain protected
author: Rodica Culcer
